label: macOS Slow — Resource Optimization
thinkingSteps: Retrieving device snapshot from 30 minutes ago…
Analyzing usage patterns for the past 3 months…

diagnosticRows.label: uptime_days = 108  (threshold: > 60 days)
diagnosticRows.result: ⚠️ Memory leak likely — processes accumulate leaked memory

diagnosticRows.label: max_cpu_usage = 98.5%  with 24 active apps / 47 Chrome tabs
diagnosticRows.result: ⚠️ High CPU load — too many open apps

rootCauses.label: Device Uptime — Memory Leak
rootCauses.evidence: 108-day uptime causes processes to accumulate leaked memory. uptime_days of 108 is 7× above the recommended cycle (< 14 days). Normal restart clears leaked allocations and typically drops RAM utilization from 91% to < 50%. Closing the lid does NOT restart — it suspends.
rootCauses.benchmark: Device uptime is longer than 94% of MacBook Pros in your org

rootCauses.label: Full Disk Space
rootCauses.evidence: macOS requires ≥ 10% free disk space for virtual memory paging and temp writes. Slow boot time (p90 = 52s vs. < 30s threshold) and high max_memory_usage confirm disk-based swap is constrained, amplifying the memory leak effect.
rootCauses.benchmark: Boot time is slower than 89% of 1 TB MacBook Pros in your org

rootCauses.label: High Memory Pressure
rootCauses.evidence: Elevated but secondary — this is a symptom of the uptime-driven leak, not an independent cause. CPU remains partially active (avg_processor_time = 42.1%), distinguishing from pure swap thrashing. Closing Chrome tabs provides temporary relief.
rootCauses.benchmark: RAM pressure is higher than 78% of 32 GB MacBook Pros in your org

benchmarkSummary.interpretation: Your laptop's RAM and boot time are significantly higher than similar MacBook Pros. The 108-day uptime is causing memory to accumulate, which forces the disk into a bottleneck. A restart resolves this immediately.

restart.title: Restart My MacBook
restart.body:
Note: Closing the lid does not restart your laptop — it only puts it to sleep.

  1. Click the Apple menu 🍎 in the top-left corner.
  2. Select Restart…
  3. Save any open files when prompted.
  4. Click Restart in the confirmation dialog.
  5. Allow the laptop to fully shut down and start back up.

Your laptop will reconnect to network resources automatically on startup.

ram.title: Optimize RAM
ram.body:
Every open tab and app consumes memory, even when idle.

  1. Click Go › Utilities at the top of your screen.
  2. Open Activity Monitor → click the Memory tab.
  3. Sort by memory usage. Identify apps you're not actively using.
  4. Double-click an app → click Quit.

disk.title: Free Up Disk Space
disk.body:
macOS needs at least 10% free space for virtual memory and temp files.

  • Find and remove files taking up space →
  • Back up your data to a network folder →
